main_event: MSGT James G. Peters observed white star-sized object flashing four times as it traversed 100 degrees of arc (170 to 350 degree azimuth, passing zenith) in four seconds from Westover AFB, Massachusetts at 0133Z on 13 July 1962.
sighted_object: white, star-sized; four evenly spaced flashes as it moved; not visible between flashes; straight path; disappeared in haze; not visible at rest
course: 350 dgr (170 dgr azimuth to 350 dgr azimuth, passing zenith)
witness_description: MSGT James G. Peters, MAC 99 Bomb Wing WAFB, stable and calm reliable observer, 17 years active duty; investigator TSGT M. Lieberman, NCOIC, Combat Intelligence Branch, Int. Div. 99 Bomb Wing
interesting_points: Observer believed sighting consistent with satellite booster in near polar orbit; not Echo 1; said he had seen one previously connected with a known satellite
Investigator (TSGT Lieberman) inclined to agree with satellite booster estimate for lack of evidence to contrary
ATIC conclusion: meteor — arc of 100 degrees in 4 seconds puts speed of object in meteor class
Weather: hazy with broken clouds, visibility 6 miles, smoke and haze; object appeared only during breaks in clouds
CSV flag A:witnesses=17000 was haiku hallucination; only 1 witness (MSGT Peters)
